name: Fit Log-Pearson Type III distribution and estimate flood return periods
prompt: Read annual_peaks.csv with 30 years of annual peak streamflow data. Fit a Log-Pearson Type III distribution: (1) compute log10 of all peak flows, (2) calculate mean, standard deviation, and skewness of the log values, (3) use frequency factors to compute flows for 10, 50, and 100-year return periods. Standard frequency factors for skew≈0: K_10=1.282, K_50=2.054, K_100=2.326. Formula: Q = 10^(mean_log + K * std_log). Write return_periods.json with: log_stats (mean, std, skew), return_periods ({rp_10, rp_50, rp_100} as flow values).

criteria.description: return_periods.json exists and is valid JSON
Log standard deviation is correct (within 0.03 of 0.20)
10-year return period flow is within 10% of expected
50-year return period flow is within 10% of expected
100-year return period flow is within 10% of expected

origin.notes: auto-converted from legacy skill-bench TaskDefinition by tools/convert-from-skill-bench.ts
